name: Leonese
notes: Leonese (Llionés), spoken in the provinces of León, Zamora, and Salamanca. Southern continuation of Asturleonese. Shares core features with Asturian (diphthongs, betacism, palatal [ʎ], ye copula) but under heavier Castilian pressure. Some Zamoran varieties show aspiration of velar fricatives. Related to Mirandese (Portugal) which descends from the same Leonese branch.

sources.author: García Arias, X.Ll.
sources.title: Gramática histórica de la lengua asturiana

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language
